description: Manually defined OpenAPI spec for endpoints being migrated to spec-first approach

tools.name: account_get-api-limits
tools.description: Endpoint to retrieve the API tier limits associated with the authenticated user. Returns the GetAccountApiLimitsResponse.

tools.name: account_get-endpoint-costs
tools.description: Lists API v2 endpoints whose configured token cost differs from the default cost. Endpoints that use the default cost are omitted. Returns the GetAccountEndpointCostsResponse.

tools.name: api-keys_create
tools.description: Endpoint for creating a new API key with a user-provided public key. This endpoint allows users with Premier or Market Maker API usage levels to create API keys by providing their own RSA public key. The platform will use this public key to verify signatures on API requests. Required: name, public_key. Optional: scopes. Returns the new CreateApiKeyResponse.

params.name: name
params.description: Name for the API key. This helps identify the key's purpose

params.name: public_key
params.description: RSA public key in PEM format. This will be used to verify signatures on API requests

params.name: scopes
params.description: List of scopes to grant to the API key. Valid values are 'read' and 'write'. If 'write' is included, 'read' must also be included. Defaults to full access (['read', 'write']) if not provided.

tools.name: api-keys_delete
tools.description: Endpoint for deleting an existing API key. This endpoint permanently deletes an API key. Once deleted, the key can no longer be used for authentication. This action cannot be undone. Required: api_key. Destructive.

params.name: api_key
params.description: API key ID to delete

tools.name: api-keys_generate
tools.description: Endpoint for generating a new API key with an automatically created key pair. This endpoint generates both a public and private RSA key pair. The public key is stored on the platform, while the private key is returned to the user and must be stored securely. The private key cannot be retrieved again. Required: name. Optional: scopes. Returns the new GenerateApiKeyResponse.

params.name: name
params.description: Name for the API key. This helps identify the key's purpose

params.name: scopes
params.description: List of scopes to grant to the API key. Valid values are 'read' and 'write'. If 'write' is included, 'read' must also be included. Defaults to full access (['read', 'write']) if not provided.

tools.name: api-keys_get
tools.description: Endpoint for retrieving all API keys associated with the authenticated user. API keys allow programmatic access to the platform without requiring username/password authentication. Each key has a unique identifier and name. Returns the GetApiKeysResponse.

tools.name: communications_accept-quote
tools.description: Endpoint for accepting a quote. This will require the quoter to confirm. Required: quote_id, accepted_side.

params.name: quote_id
params.description: Quote ID

params.name: accepted_side
params.description: The side of the quote to accept (yes or no)

tools.name: communications_confirm-quote
tools.description: Endpoint for confirming a quote. This will start a timer for order execution. Required: quote_id.

params.name: quote_id
params.description: Quote ID

tools.name: communications_create-quote
tools.description: Endpoint for creating a quote in response to an RFQ. Required: no_bid, rest_remainder, rfq_id, yes_bid. Optional: subaccount. Returns the new CreateQuoteResponse.

params.name: no_bid
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

params.name: rest_remainder
params.description: Whether to rest the remainder of the quote after execution

params.name: rfq_id
params.description: The ID of the RFQ to quote on

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Optional subaccount number to place the quote under (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts)

params.name: yes_bid
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

tools.name: communications_create-rfq
tools.description: Endpoint for creating a new RFQ. You can have a maximum of 100 open RFQs at a time. Required: market_ticker, rest_remainder. Optional: contracts, contracts_fp, replace_existing (default: false) (plus 4 more). Returns the new CreateRfqresponse.

params.name: contracts
params.description: The number of contracts for the RFQ. Whole contracts only. Contracts may be provided via contracts or contracts_fp; if both provided they must match.

params.name: contracts_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., '10.00'; referred to as 'fp' in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., '10', '10.0', '10.00'); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., '2.50') are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: market_ticker
params.description: The ticker of the market for which to create an RFQ

params.name: replace_existing
params.description: Whether to delete existing RFQs as part of this RFQ's creation

params.name: rest_remainder
params.description: Whether to rest the remainder of the RFQ after execution

params.name: subaccount
params.description: The subaccount number to create the RFQ for (direct members only; 0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts)

params.name: subtrader_id
params.description: The subtrader to create the RFQ for (FCM members only)

params.name: target_cost_centi_cents
params.description: DEPRECATED: The target cost for the RFQ in centi-cents. Use target_cost_dollars instead.

params.name: target_cost_dollars
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

tools.name: communications_delete-quote
tools.description: Endpoint for deleting a quote, which means it can no longer be accepted. Required: quote_id. Destructive.

params.name: quote_id
params.description: Quote ID

tools.name: communications_delete-rfq
tools.description: Endpoint for deleting an RFQ by ID. Required: rfq_id. Destructive.

params.name: rfq_id
params.description: RFQ ID

tools.name: communications_get-id
tools.description: Endpoint for getting the communications ID of the logged-in user. Returns the GetCommunicationsIdresponse.

tools.name: communications_get-quote
tools.description: Endpoint for getting a particular quote. Required: quote_id. Returns the GetQuoteResponse.

params.name: quote_id
params.description: Quote ID

tools.name: communications_get-quotes
tools.description: Endpoint for getting quotes. Optional: cursor, event_ticker, market_ticker (plus 7 more). Returns the GetQuotesResponse.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker to filter by. Only a single event ticker is supported.

params.name: market_ticker
params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: limit
params.description: Parameter to specify the number of results per page. Defaults to 500.

params.name: status
params.description: Filter quotes by status

params.name: quote_creator_user_id
params.description: Filter quotes by quote creator user ID

params.name: user_filter
params.description: User filter

params.name: rfq_creator_user_id
params.description: Filter quotes by RFQ creator user ID

params.name: rfq_creator_subtrader_id
params.description: Filter quotes by RFQ creator subtrader ID (FCM members only)

params.name: rfq_id
params.description: Filter quotes by RFQ ID

tools.name: communications_get-rfq
tools.description: Endpoint for getting a single RFQ by id. Required: rfq_id. Returns the GetRfqresponse.

params.name: rfq_id
params.description: RFQ ID

tools.name: communications_get-rfqs
tools.description: Endpoint for getting RFQs. Optional: cursor, event_ticker, market_ticker (plus 5 more). Returns the GetRfqsResponse.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker to filter by. Only a single event ticker is supported.

params.name: market_ticker
params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). If omitted, defaults to all subaccounts.

params.name: limit
params.description: Parameter to specify the number of results per page. Defaults to 100.

params.name: status
params.description: Filter RFQs by status

params.name: creator_user_id
params.description: Filter RFQs by creator user ID

params.name: user_filter
params.description: User filter

tools.name: events_get
tools.description: Get all events. This endpoint excludes multivariate events. To retrieve multivariate events, use the GET /events/multivariate endpoint. All events are accessible through this endpoint, even if their associated markets are older than the historical cutoff. Optional: limit (default: 200), cursor, with_nested_markets (default: false) (plus 5 more). Returns the GetEventsResponse.

params.name: limit
params.description: Parameter to specify the number of results per page. Defaults to 200. Maximum value is 200.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Parameter to specify the pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next page of results. Leave empty for the first page.

params.name: with_nested_markets
params.description: Parameter to specify if nested markets should be included in the response. When true, each event will include a 'markets' field containing a list of Market objects associated with that event. Historical markets settled before the historical cutoff will not be included.

params.name: with_milestones
params.description: If true, includes related milestones as a field alongside events.

params.name: status
params.description: Filter by event status. Possible values are 'unopened', 'open', 'closed', 'settled'. Leave empty to return events with any status.

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: Filter by series ticker

params.name: min_close_ts
params.description: Filter events with at least one market with close timestamp greater than this Unix timestamp (in seconds).

params.name: min_updated_ts
params.description: Filter events with metadata updated after this Unix timestamp (in seconds). Use this to efficiently poll for changes.

tools.name: events_get-eventticker
tools.description: Endpoint for getting data about an event by its ticker. An event represents a real-world occurrence that can be traded on, such as an election, sports game, or economic indicator release. Events contain one or more markets where users can place trades on different outcomes. All events are accessible through this endpoint, even if their associated markets are older than the historical cutoff. Required: event_ticker. Optional: with_nested_markets (default: false). Returns the GetEventResponse.

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker

params.name: with_nested_markets
params.description: If true, markets are included within the event object. If false (default), markets are returned as a separate top-level field in the response. Historical markets settled before the historical cutoff will not be included.

tools.name: events_get-multivariate
tools.description: Retrieve multivariate (combo) events. These are dynamically created events from multivariate event collections. Supports filtering by series and collection ticker. Optional: limit (default: 100), cursor, series_ticker (plus 2 more). Returns the GetMultivariateEventsResponse.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100. Maximum value is 200.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next page of results.

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: Filter by series ticker

params.name: collection_ticker
params.description: Filter events by collection ticker. Returns only multivariate events belonging to the specified collection. Cannot be used together with series_ticker.

params.name: with_nested_markets
params.description: Parameter to specify if nested markets should be included in the response. When true, each event will include a 'markets' field containing a list of Market objects associated with that event.

tools.name: events_metadata_get-event
tools.description: Endpoint for getting metadata about an event by its ticker. Returns only the metadata information for an event. Required: event_ticker.

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker

tools.name: exchange_get-announcements
tools.description: Endpoint for getting all exchange-wide announcements. Returns the GetExchangeAnnouncementsResponse.

tools.name: exchange_get-schedule
tools.description: Endpoint for getting the exchange schedule. Returns the GetExchangeScheduleResponse.

tools.name: exchange_get-status
tools.description: Endpoint for getting the exchange status. Returns the ExchangeStatus.

tools.name: exchange_get-user-data-timestamp
tools.description: There is typically a short delay before exchange events are reflected in the API endpoints. Whenever possible, combine API responses to PUT/POST/DELETE requests with websocket data to obtain the most accurate view of the exchange state. This endpoint provides an approximate indication of when the data from the following endpoints was last validated: GetBalance, GetOrder(s), GetFills, GetPositions. Returns the GetUserDataTimestampResponse.

tools.name: fcm_get-fcmorders
tools.description: Endpoint for FCM members to get orders filtered by subtrader ID. This endpoint requires FCM member access level and allows filtering orders by subtrader ID. Required: subtrader_id. Optional: cursor, event_ticker, ticker (plus 4 more). Returns the GetOrdersResponse.

params.name: subtrader_id
params.description: Restricts the response to orders for a specific subtrader (FCM members only)

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker to filter by. Only a single event ticker is supported.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: min_ts
params.description: Restricts the response to orders after a timestamp, formatted as a Unix Timestamp

params.name: max_ts
params.description: Restricts the response to orders before a timestamp, formatted as a Unix Timestamp

params.name: status
params.description: Restricts the response to orders that have a certain status

params.name: limit
params.description: Parameter to specify the number of results per page. Defaults to 100

tools.name: fcm_get-fcmpositions
tools.description: Endpoint for FCM members to get market positions filtered by subtrader ID. This endpoint requires FCM member access level and allows filtering positions by subtrader ID. Required: subtrader_id. Optional: ticker, event_ticker, count_filter (plus 3 more). Returns the GetPositionsResponse.

params.name: subtrader_id
params.description: Restricts the response to positions for a specific subtrader (FCM members only)

params.name: ticker
params.description: Ticker of desired positions

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker of desired positions

params.name: count_filter
params.description: Restricts the positions to those with any of following fields with non-zero values, as a comma separated list

params.name: settlement_status
params.description: Settlement status of the markets to return. Defaults to unsettled

params.name: limit
params.description: Parameter to specify the number of results per page. Defaults to 100

params.name: cursor
params.description: The Cursor represents a pointer to the next page of records in the pagination

tools.name: historical_get-cutoff
tools.description: Returns the cutoff timestamps that define the boundary between **live** and **historical** data. ## Cutoff fields - `market_settled_ts` : Markets that **settled** before this timestamp, and their candlesticks, must be accessed via `GET /historical/markets` and `GET /historical/markets/{ticker}/candlesticks`. - `trades_created_ts` : Trades that were **filled** before this timestamp must be accessed via `GET /historical/fills`. - `orders_updated_ts` : Orders that were **canceled or fully executed** before this timestamp must be accessed via `GET /historical/orders`. Resting (active) orders are always available in `GET /portfolio/orders`.

tools.name: historical_get-fills
tools.description: Endpoint for getting all historical fills for the member. A fill is when a trade you have is matched. Optional: ticker, max_ts, limit (default: 100) (plus 1 more). Returns the GetFillsResponse.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: max_ts
params.description: Filter items before this Unix timestamp

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

tools.name: historical_get-market
tools.description: Endpoint for getting data about a specific market by its ticker from the historical database. Required: ticker. Returns the GetMarketResponse.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Market ticker

tools.name: historical_get-market-candlesticks
tools.description: Endpoint for fetching historical candlestick data for markets that have been archived from the live data set. Time period length of each candlestick in minutes. Valid values: 1 (1 minute), 60 (1 hour), 1440 (1 day). Required: ticker, start_ts, end_ts, period_interval. Returns the GetMarketCandlesticksHistoricalResponse.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Market ticker - unique identifier for the specific market

params.name: start_ts
params.description: Start timestamp (Unix timestamp). Candlesticks will include those ending on or after this time.

params.name: end_ts
params.description: End timestamp (Unix timestamp). Candlesticks will include those ending on or before this time.

params.name: period_interval
params.description: Time period length of each candlestick in minutes. Valid values are 1 (1 minute), 60 (1 hour), or 1440 (1 day).

tools.name: historical_get-markets
tools.description: Endpoint for getting markets that have been archived to the historical database. Filters are mutually exclusive. Optional: limit (default: 100), cursor, tickers (plus 3 more). Returns the GetMarketsResponse.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100. Maximum value is 1000.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

params.name: tickers
params.description: Filter by specific market tickers. Comma-separated list of market tickers to retrieve.

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker to filter by. Only a single event ticker is supported.

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: Filter by series ticker

params.name: mve_filter
params.description: Filter by multivariate events (combos). By default, MVE markets are included.

tools.name: historical_get-orders
tools.description: Endpoint for getting orders that have been archived to the historical database. Optional: ticker, max_ts, limit (default: 100) (plus 1 more). Returns the GetOrdersResponse.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: max_ts
params.description: Filter items before this Unix timestamp

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

tools.name: historical_get-trades
tools.description: Endpoint for getting all historical trades for all markets. Trades that were filled before the historical cutoff are available via this endpoint. See [Historical Data](https://kalshi.com/docs/getting_started/historical_data) for details. Optional: ticker, min_ts, max_ts (plus 2 more). Returns the GetTradesResponse.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: min_ts
params.description: Filter items after this Unix timestamp

params.name: max_ts
params.description: Filter items before this Unix timestamp

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100. Maximum value is 1000.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

tools.name: incentive-programs_get
tools.description: List incentives with optional filters. Incentives are rewards programs for trading activity on specific markets. Optional: status, type, incentive_description (plus 2 more). Returns the GetIncentiveProgramsResponse.

params.name: status
params.description: Status filter. Can be 'all', 'active', 'upcoming', 'closed', or 'paid_out'. Default is 'all'.

params.name: type
params.description: Type filter. Can be 'all', 'liquidity', or 'volume'. Default is 'all'.

params.name: incentive_description
params.description: Filter by exact incentive description.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100. Maximum value is 10000.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Cursor for pagination

tools.name: kalshi-trade-manual-search_get-filters-for-sports
tools.description: Retrieve available filters organized by sport. This endpoint returns filtering options available for each sport, including scopes and competitions. It also provides an ordered list of sports for display purposes.

tools.name: kalshi-trade-manual-search-2_get-tags-for-series-categories
tools.description: Retrieve tags organized by series categories. This endpoint returns a mapping of series categories to their associated tags, which can be used for filtering and search functionality.

tools.name: live-data_get
tools.description: Get live data for multiple milestones. Required: milestone_ids. Optional: include_player_stats (default: false). Returns the GetLiveDatasResponse.

params.name: milestone_ids
params.description: Array of milestone IDs

params.name: include_player_stats
params.description: When true, includes player-level statistics in the live data response.

tools.name: live-data_get-by-milestone
tools.description: Get live data for a specific milestone. Required: milestone_id. Optional: include_player_stats (default: false). Returns the GetLiveDataResponse.

params.name: milestone_id
params.description: Milestone ID

params.name: include_player_stats
params.description: When true, includes player-level statistics in the live data response.

tools.name: live-data_get-game-stats
tools.description: Get play-by-play game statistics for a specific milestone. Supported sports: Pro Football, College Football, Pro Basketball, College Men's Basketball, College Women's Basketball, WNBA, Soccer, Pro Hockey, and Pro Baseball. Returns null for unsupported milestone types or milestones without a Sportradar ID. Required: milestone_id.

params.name: milestone_id
params.description: Milestone ID

tools.name: live-data_milestone_get-live-data
tools.description: Get live data for a specific milestone. This is the legacy endpoint that requires a type path parameter. Prefer using `/live_data/milestone/{milestone_id}` instead. Required: type, milestone_id. Optional: include_player_stats (default: false). Returns the GetLiveDataResponse.

params.name: type
params.description: Type of live data

params.name: milestone_id
params.description: Milestone ID

params.name: include_player_stats
params.description: When true, includes player-level statistics in the live data response.

tools.name: markets_batch-get-candlesticks
tools.description: Endpoint for retrieving candlestick data for multiple markets. - Accepts up to 100 market tickers per request - Returns up to 10,000 candlesticks total across all markets - Returns candlesticks grouped by market_id - Optionally includes a synthetic initial candlestick for price continuity (see `include_latest_before_start` parameter). Required: market_tickers, start_ts, end_ts, period_interval. Optional: include_latest_before_start (default: false).

params.name: market_tickers
params.description: Comma-separated list of market tickers (maximum 100)

params.name: start_ts
params.description: Start timestamp in Unix seconds

params.name: end_ts
params.description: End timestamp in Unix seconds

params.name: period_interval
params.description: Candlestick period interval in minutes

params.name: include_latest_before_start
params.description: If true, prepends the latest candlestick available before the start_ts. This synthetic candlestick is created by: 1. Finding the most recent real candlestick before start_ts 2. Projecting it forward to the first period boundary (calculated as the next period interval after start_ts) 3. Setting all OHLC prices to null, and `previous_price` to the close price from the real candlestick

tools.name: markets_get
tools.description: Filter by market status. Possible values: `unopened`, `open`, `closed`, `settled`. Leave empty to return markets with any status. - Only one `status` filter may be supplied at a time. - Timestamp filters will be mutually exclusive from other timestamp filters and certain status filters. | Compatible Timestamp Filters | Additional Status Filters| Extra Notes | |------------------------------|--------------------------|-------------| | min_created_ts, max_created_ts | `unopened`, `open`, *empty* | | | min_close_ts, max_close_ts | `closed`, *empty* | | | min_settled_ts, max_settled_ts | `settled`, *empty* | | | min_updated_ts | *empty* | Incompatible with all filters besides `mve_filter=exclude` | Markets that settled before the historical cutoff are only available via `GET /historical/markets`. See [Historical Data](https://kalshi.com/docs/getting_started/historical_data) for details. Optional: limit (default: 100), cursor, event_ticker (plus 11 more). Returns the GetMarketsResponse.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100. Maximum value is 1000.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker to filter by. Only a single event ticker is supported.

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: Filter by series ticker

params.name: min_created_ts
params.description: Filter items that created after this Unix timestamp

params.name: max_created_ts
params.description: Filter items that created before this Unix timestamp

params.name: min_updated_ts
params.description: Return markets with metadata updated later than this Unix timestamp. Tracks non-trading changes only. Incompatible with any other filters.

params.name: max_close_ts
params.description: Filter items that close before this Unix timestamp

params.name: min_close_ts
params.description: Filter items that close after this Unix timestamp

params.name: min_settled_ts
params.description: Filter items that settled after this Unix timestamp

params.name: max_settled_ts
params.description: Filter items that settled before this Unix timestamp

params.name: status
params.description: Filter by market status. Leave empty to return markets with any status.

params.name: tickers
params.description: Filter by specific market tickers. Comma-separated list of market tickers to retrieve.

params.name: mve_filter
params.description: Filter by multivariate events (combos). 'only' returns only multivariate events, 'exclude' excludes multivariate events.

tools.name: markets_get-orderbooks
tools.description: Endpoint for getting the current order books for multiple markets in a single request. The order book shows all active bid orders for both yes and no sides of a binary market. It returns yes bids and no bids only (no asks are returned). This is because in binary markets, a bid for yes at price X is equivalent to an ask for no at price (100-X). For example, a yes bid at 7¢ is the same as a no ask at 93¢, with identical contract sizes. Each side shows price levels with their corresponding quantities and order counts, organized from best to worst prices. Returns one orderbook per requested market ticker. Required: tickers.

params.name: tickers
params.description: List of market tickers to fetch orderbooks for

tools.name: markets_get-ticker
tools.description: Endpoint for getting data about a specific market by its ticker. A market represents a specific binary outcome within an event that users can trade on (e.g., 'Will candidate X win?'). Markets have yes/no positions, current prices, volume, and settlement rules. Required: ticker. Returns the GetMarketResponse.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Market ticker

tools.name: markets_get-trades
tools.description: Endpoint for getting all trades for all markets. A trade represents a completed transaction between two users on a specific market. Each trade includes the market ticker, price, quantity, and timestamp information. This endpoint returns a paginated response. Use the 'limit' parameter to control page size (1-1000, defaults to 100). The response includes a 'cursor' field - pass this value in the 'cursor' parameter of your next request to get the next page. An empty cursor indicates no more pages are available. Optional: limit (default: 100), cursor, ticker (plus 2 more).

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100. Maximum value is 1000.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

params.name: ticker
params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: min_ts
params.description: Filter items after this Unix timestamp

params.name: max_ts
params.description: Filter items before this Unix timestamp

tools.name: markets_orderbook_get-market
tools.description: Endpoint for getting the current order book for a specific market. The order book shows all active bid orders for both yes and no sides of a binary market. It returns yes bids and no bids only (no asks are returned). This is because in binary markets, a bid for yes at price X is equivalent to an ask for no at price (100-X). For example, a yes bid at 7¢ is the same as a no ask at 93¢, with identical contract sizes. Each side shows price levels with their corresponding quantities and order counts, organized from best to worst prices. Required: ticker. Optional: depth (default: 0).

params.name: ticker
params.description: Market ticker

params.name: depth
params.description: Depth of the orderbook to retrieve (0 or negative means all levels, 1-100 for specific depth)

tools.name: milestones_get
tools.description: Minimum start date to filter milestones. Format: RFC3339 timestamp. Required: limit. Optional: minimum_start_date, category, competition (plus 5 more). Returns the GetMilestonesResponse.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of milestones to return per page

params.name: minimum_start_date
params.description: Minimum start date to filter milestones. Format RFC3339 timestamp

params.name: category
params.description: Filter by milestone category. E.g. Sports, Elections, Esports, Crypto.

params.name: competition
params.description: Filter by competition. E.g. Pro Football, Pro Basketball (M), Pro Baseball, Pro Hockey, College Football.

params.name: source_id
params.description: Filter by source id

params.name: type
params.description: Filter by milestone type. E.g. football_game, basketball_game, soccer_tournament_multi_leg, baseball_game, hockey_match, political_race.

params.name: related_event_ticker
params.description: Filter by related event ticker

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next page of results

params.name: min_updated_ts
params.description: Filter milestones with metadata updated after this Unix timestamp (in seconds). Use this to efficiently poll for changes.

tools.name: milestones_get-milestoneid
tools.description: Endpoint for getting data about a specific milestone by its ID. Required: milestone_id. Returns the GetMilestoneResponse.

params.name: milestone_id
params.description: Milestone ID

tools.name: multivariate-event-collections_create-market-in
tools.description: Endpoint for creating an individual market in a multivariate event collection. This endpoint must be hit at least once before trading or looking up a market. Users are limited to 5000 creations per week. Required: collection_ticker. Optional: with_market_payload. Returns the new CreateMarketInMultivariateEventCollectionResponse.

params.name: collection_ticker
params.description: Collection ticker

params.name: with_market_payload
params.description: Whether to include the market payload in the response.

tools.name: multivariate-event-collections_get
tools.description: Endpoint for getting data about multivariate event collections. Optional: status, associated_event_ticker, series_ticker (plus 2 more). Returns the GetMultivariateEventCollectionsResponse.

params.name: status
params.description: Only return collections of a certain status. Can be unopened, open, or closed.

params.name: associated_event_ticker
params.description: Only return collections associated with a particular event ticker.

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: Only return collections with a particular series ticker.

params.name: limit
params.description: Specify the maximum number of results.

params.name: cursor
params.description: The Cursor represents a pointer to the next page of records in the pagination. This optional parameter, when filled, should be filled with the cursor string returned in a previous request to this end-point.

tools.name: multivariate-event-collections_get-multivariateeventcollections
tools.description: Endpoint for getting data about a multivariate event collection by its ticker. Required: collection_ticker. Returns the GetMultivariateEventCollectionResponse.

params.name: collection_ticker
params.description: Collection ticker

tools.name: multivariate-event-collections_lookup_get-multivariate-event-collection-history
tools.description: DEPRECATED: This endpoint predates RFQs and should not be used for new integrations. Endpoint for retrieving which markets in an event collection were recently looked up. Required: collection_ticker, lookback_seconds. Returns the GetMultivariateEventCollectionLookupHistoryResponse.

params.name: collection_ticker
params.description: Collection ticker

params.name: lookback_seconds
params.description: Number of seconds to look back for lookup history. Must be one of 10, 60, 300, or 3600.

tools.name: multivariate-event-collections_lookup_tickers-for-market-in-multivariate-event-collection
tools.description: DEPRECATED: This endpoint predates RFQs and should not be used for new integrations. Endpoint for looking up an individual market in a multivariate event collection. If CreateMarketInMultivariateEventCollection has never been hit with that variable combination before, this will return a 404. Required: collection_ticker. Returns the updated LookupTickersForMarketInMultivariateEventCollectionResponse.

params.name: collection_ticker
params.description: Collection ticker

tools.name: portfolio_amend-order
tools.description: Endpoint for amending the max number of fillable contracts and/or price in an existing order. Max fillable contracts is `remaining_count` + `fill_count`. Required: order_id, action, side, ticker. Optional: client_order_id, count, count_fp (plus 6 more). Returns the new AmendOrderResponse.

params.name: order_id
params.description: Order ID

params.name: action
params.description: Action of the order

params.name: client_order_id
params.description: The original client-specified order ID to be amended

params.name: count
params.description: Updated quantity for the order (whole contracts only). If updating quantity, provide count or count_fp; if both provided they must match.

params.name: count_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., '10.00'; referred to as 'fp' in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., '10', '10.0', '10.00'); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., '2.50') are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: no_price
params.description: Updated no price for the order in cents

params.name: no_price_dollars
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

params.name: side
params.description: Side of the order

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Optional subaccount number to use for this amendment (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts)

params.name: ticker
params.description: Market ticker

params.name: updated_client_order_id
params.description: The new client-specified order ID after amendment

params.name: yes_price
params.description: Updated yes price for the order in cents

params.name: yes_price_dollars
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

tools.name: portfolio_amend-order-v2
tools.description: Endpoint for amending the price and/or remaining count of an existing event-market order using the V2 request/response shape. Required: order_id, count, price, side, ticker. Optional: subaccount, client_order_id, updated_client_order_id. Returns the new AmendOrderV2Response.

params.name: order_id
params.description: Order ID

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

params.name: client_order_id
params.description: The original client-specified order ID to be amended

params.name: count
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., '10.00'; referred to as 'fp' in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., '10', '10.0', '10.00'); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., '2.50') are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: price
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

params.name: side
params.description: Side of the book for an order or trade. For event markets, this refers to the YES leg only: `bid` means buy YES, `ask` means sell YES. (Selling YES is economically equivalent to buying NO at `1 - price`, but this endpoint quotes everything from the YES side.)

params.name: ticker
params.description: Market ticker

params.name: updated_client_order_id
params.description: The new client-specified order ID after amendment

tools.name: portfolio_apply-subaccount-transfer
tools.description: Transfers funds between the authenticated user's subaccounts. Use 0 for the primary account, or 1-32 for numbered subaccounts. Required: amount_cents, client_transfer_id, from_subaccount, to_subaccount. Returns the new ApplySubaccountTransferResponse.

params.name: amount_cents
params.description: Amount to transfer in cents.

params.name: client_transfer_id
params.description: Unique client-provided transfer ID for idempotency.

params.name: from_subaccount
params.description: Source subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for numbered subaccounts).

params.name: to_subaccount
params.description: Destination subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for numbered subaccounts).

tools.name: portfolio_batch-cancel-orders
tools.description: Endpoint for cancelling a batch of orders. The maximum batch size scales with your tier's write budget — see [Rate Limits and Tiers](/getting_started/rate_limits). Optional: ids. Returns the BatchCancelOrdersResponse. Destructive.

params.name: ids
params.description: An array of order IDs to cancel

tools.name: portfolio_batch-cancel-orders-v2
tools.description: Endpoint for cancelling a batch of event-market orders using the V2 response shape. The maximum batch size scales with your tier's write budget — see [Rate Limits and Tiers](/getting_started/rate_limits). Returns the BatchCancelOrdersV2Response. Destructive.

tools.name: portfolio_batch-create-orders
tools.description: Endpoint for submitting a batch of orders. The maximum batch size scales with your tier's write budget — see [Rate Limits and Tiers](/getting_started/rate_limits). Returns the new BatchCreateOrdersResponse.

tools.name: portfolio_batch-create-orders-v2
tools.description: Endpoint for submitting a batch of event-market orders using the V2 request/response shape. The maximum batch size scales with your tier's write budget — see [Rate Limits and Tiers](/getting_started/rate_limits). Returns the new BatchCreateOrdersV2Response.

tools.name: portfolio_cancel-order
tools.description: Endpoint for canceling orders. The value for the orderId should match the id field of the order you want to decrease. Commonly, DELETE-type endpoints return 204 status with no body content on success. But we can't completely delete the order, as it may be partially filled already. Instead, the DeleteOrder endpoint reduce the order completely, essentially zeroing the remaining resting contracts on it. The zeroed order is returned on the response payload as a form of validation for the client. Required: order_id. Optional: subaccount. Returns the CancelOrderResponse. Destructive.

params.name: order_id
params.description: Order ID

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: portfolio_cancel-order-v2
tools.description: Endpoint for cancelling event-market orders using the V2 response shape. Returns `{order_id, client_order_id, reduced_by}` rather than a full order object. Required: order_id. Optional: subaccount. Destructive.

params.name: order_id
params.description: Order ID

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: portfolio_create-order
tools.description: Endpoint for submitting orders in a market. Each user is limited to 200 000 open orders at a time. Required: action, side, ticker. Optional: buy_max_cost, cancel_order_on_pause, client_order_id (plus 13 more). Returns the new CreateOrderResponse.

params.name: action
params.description: Action

params.name: buy_max_cost
params.description: Maximum cost in cents. When specified, the order will automatically have Fill-or-Kill (FoK) behavior.

params.name: cancel_order_on_pause
params.description: If this flag is set to true, the order will be canceled if the order is open and trading on the exchange is paused for any reason.

params.name: client_order_id
params.description: Client order id

params.name: count
params.description: Order quantity in contracts (whole contracts only). Provide count or count_fp; if both provided they must match.

params.name: count_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., '10.00'; referred to as 'fp' in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., '10', '10.0', '10.00'); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., '2.50') are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: expiration_ts
params.description: Expiration ts

params.name: no_price
params.description: No price

params.name: no_price_dollars
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

params.name: order_group_id
params.description: The order group this order is part of

params.name: post_only
params.description: Post only

params.name: reduce_only
params.description: Reduce only

params.name: sell_position_floor
params.description: Deprecated: Use reduce_only instead. Only accepts value of 0.

params.name: side
params.description: Side

params.name: subaccount
params.description: The subaccount number to use for this order. 0 is the primary subaccount.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Ticker

params.name: time_in_force
params.description: Time in force

params.name: yes_price
params.description: Yes price

params.name: yes_price_dollars
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

tools.name: portfolio_create-order-group
tools.description: Creates a new order group with a contracts limit measured over a rolling 15-second window. When the limit is hit, all orders in the group are cancelled and no new orders can be placed until reset. Optional: contracts_limit, contracts_limit_fp, subaccount (default: 0). Returns the new CreateOrderGroupResponse.

params.name: contracts_limit
params.description: Specifies the maximum number of contracts that can be matched within this group over a rolling 15-second window. Whole contracts only. Provide contracts_limit or contracts_limit_fp; if both provided they must match.

params.name: contracts_limit_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., '10.00'; referred to as 'fp' in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., '10', '10.0', '10.00'); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., '2.50') are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Optional subaccount number to use for this order group (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts)

tools.name: portfolio_create-order-v2
tools.description: Endpoint for submitting event-market orders using the V2 request/response shape (single-book `bid`/`ask` side and fixed-point dollar prices). The legacy `/portfolio/orders` endpoint will be deprecated no earlier than May 6, 2026 — clients should migrate to this path. Required: client_order_id, count, price, side, ticker, time_in_force. Optional: cancel_order_on_pause, expiration_time, order_group_id (plus 3 more). Returns the new CreateOrderV2Response.

params.name: cancel_order_on_pause
params.description: If this flag is set to true, the order will be canceled if the order is open and trading on the exchange is paused for any reason.

params.name: client_order_id
params.description: Client order id

params.name: count
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., '10.00'; referred to as 'fp' in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., '10', '10.0', '10.00'); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., '2.50') are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: expiration_time
params.description: Expiration time

params.name: order_group_id
params.description: The order group this order is part of

params.name: post_only
params.description: Post only

params.name: price
params.description: US dollar amount as a fixed-point decimal string with up to 6 decimal places of precision. This is the maximum supported precision; valid quote intervals for a given market are constrained by that market's price level structure.

params.name: reduce_only
params.description: Specifies whether the order place count should be capped by the member's current position.

params.name: side
params.description: Side of the book for an order or trade. For event markets, this refers to the YES leg only: `bid` means buy YES, `ask` means sell YES. (Selling YES is economically equivalent to buying NO at `1 - price`, but this endpoint quotes everything from the YES side.)

params.name: subaccount
params.description: The subaccount number to use for this order. 0 is the primary subaccount.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Ticker

params.name: time_in_force
params.description: Time in force

tools.name: portfolio_create-subaccount
tools.description: Creates a new subaccount for the authenticated user. Subaccounts are numbered sequentially starting from 1. Maximum 32 subaccounts per user. Returns the new CreateSubaccountResponse.

tools.name: portfolio_decrease-order
tools.description: Endpoint for decreasing the number of contracts in an existing order. This is the only kind of edit available on order quantity. Cancelling an order is equivalent to decreasing an order amount to zero. Required: order_id. Optional: reduce_by, reduce_by_fp, reduce_to (plus 2 more). Returns the new DecreaseOrderResponse.

params.name: order_id
params.description: Order ID

params.name: reduce_by
params.description: Number of contracts to reduce by (whole contracts only). Reduce-by may be provided via reduce_by or reduce_by_fp; if both provided they must match. Exactly one of reduce_by(/reduce_by_fp) or reduce_to(/reduce_to_fp) must be provided.

params.name: reduce_by_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., '10.00'; referred to as 'fp' in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., '10', '10.0', '10.00'); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., '2.50') are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: reduce_to
params.description: Number of contracts to reduce to (whole contracts only). Reduce-to may be provided via reduce_to or reduce_to_fp; if both provided they must match. Exactly one of reduce_by(/reduce_by_fp) or reduce_to(/reduce_to_fp) must be provided.

params.name: reduce_to_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., '10.00'; referred to as 'fp' in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., '10', '10.0', '10.00'); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., '2.50') are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Optional subaccount number to use for this decrease (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts)

tools.name: portfolio_decrease-order-v2
tools.description: Endpoint for decreasing the remaining count of an existing event-market order using the V2 request/response shape. Only `reduce_to` is supported. Required: order_id, reduce_to. Optional: subaccount. Returns the new DecreaseOrderV2Response.

params.name: order_id
params.description: Order ID

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

params.name: reduce_to
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., '10.00'; referred to as 'fp' in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., '10', '10.0', '10.00'); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., '2.50') are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

tools.name: portfolio_delete-order-group
tools.description: Deletes an order group and cancels all orders within it. This permanently removes the group. Required: order_group_id. Optional: subaccount. Returns the EmptyResponse. Destructive.

params.name: order_group_id
params.description: Order group ID

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: portfolio_get-balance
tools.description: Endpoint for getting the balance and portfolio value of a member. Both values are returned in cents. Optional: subaccount. Returns the GetBalanceResponse.

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: portfolio_get-fills
tools.description: Endpoint for getting all fills for the member. A fill is when a trade you have is matched. Fills that occurred before the historical cutoff are only available via `GET /historical/fills`. See [Historical Data](https://kalshi.com/docs/getting_started/historical_data) for details. Optional: ticker, order_id, min_ts (plus 4 more). Returns the GetFillsResponse.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: order_id
params.description: Filter by order ID

params.name: min_ts
params.description: Filter items after this Unix timestamp

params.name: max_ts
params.description: Filter items before this Unix timestamp

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). If omitted, defaults to all subaccounts.

tools.name: portfolio_get-order
tools.description: Endpoint for getting a single order. Required: order_id. Returns the GetOrderResponse.

params.name: order_id
params.description: Order ID

tools.name: portfolio_get-order-group
tools.description: Retrieves details for a single order group including all order IDs and auto-cancel status. Required: order_group_id. Optional: subaccount. Returns the GetOrderGroupResponse.

params.name: order_group_id
params.description: Order group ID

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). If omitted, defaults to all subaccounts.

tools.name: portfolio_get-order-groups
tools.description: Retrieves all order groups for the authenticated user. Optional: subaccount. Returns the GetOrderGroupsResponse.

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). If omitted, defaults to all subaccounts.

tools.name: portfolio_get-order-queue-position
tools.description: Endpoint for getting an order's queue position in the order book. This represents the amount of orders that need to be matched before this order receives a partial or full match. Queue position is determined using a price-time priority. Required: order_id. Returns the GetOrderQueuePositionResponse.

params.name: order_id
params.description: Order ID

tools.name: portfolio_get-order-queue-positions
tools.description: Endpoint for getting queue positions for all resting orders. Queue position represents the number of contracts that need to be matched before an order receives a partial or full match, determined using price-time priority. Optional: market_tickers, event_ticker, subaccount. Returns the GetOrderQueuePositionsResponse.

params.name: market_tickers
params.description: Comma-separated list of market tickers to filter by

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker to filter by

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: portfolio_get-orders
tools.description: Restricts the response to orders that have a certain status: resting, canceled, or executed. Orders that have been canceled or fully executed before the historical cutoff are only available via `GET /historical/orders`. Resting orders will always be available through this endpoint. See [Historical Data](https://kalshi.com/docs/getting_started/historical_data) for details. Optional: ticker, event_ticker, min_ts (plus 5 more). Returns the GetOrdersResponse.

params.name: ticker
params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event tickers to filter by, as a comma-separated list (maximum 10).

params.name: min_ts
params.description: Filter items after this Unix timestamp

params.name: max_ts
params.description: Filter items before this Unix timestamp

params.name: status
params.description: Filter by status. Possible values depend on the endpoint.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). If omitted, defaults to all subaccounts.

tools.name: portfolio_get-positions
tools.description: Restricts the positions to those with any of following fields with non-zero values, as a comma separated list. The following values are accepted: position, total_traded. Optional: cursor, limit (default: 100), count_filter (plus 3 more). Returns the GetPositionsResponse.

params.name: cursor
params.description: The Cursor represents a pointer to the next page of records in the pagination. Use the value returned from the previous response to get the next page.

params.name: limit
params.description: Parameter to specify the number of results per page. Defaults to 100.

params.name: count_filter
params.description: Restricts the positions to those with any of following fields with non-zero values, as a comma separated list. The following values are accepted - position, total_traded

params.name: ticker
params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker to filter by. Only a single event ticker is supported.

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: portfolio_get-resting-order-total-value
tools.description: Endpoint for getting the total value, in cents, of resting orders. This endpoint is only intended for use by FCM members (rare). Note: If you're uncertain about this endpoint, it likely does not apply to you. Returns the GetPortfolioRestingOrderTotalValueResponse.

tools.name: portfolio_get-settlements
tools.description: Endpoint for getting the member's settlements historical track. Optional: limit (default: 100), cursor, ticker (plus 4 more). Returns the GetSettlementsResponse.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

params.name: ticker
params.description: Filter by market ticker

params.name: event_ticker
params.description: Event ticker to filter by. Only a single event ticker is supported.

params.name: min_ts
params.description: Filter items after this Unix timestamp

params.name: max_ts
params.description: Filter items before this Unix timestamp

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). If omitted, defaults to all subaccounts.

tools.name: portfolio_get-subaccount-balances
tools.description: Gets balances for all subaccounts including the primary account. Returns the GetSubaccountBalancesResponse.

tools.name: portfolio_get-subaccount-netting
tools.description: Gets the netting enabled settings for all subaccounts. Returns the GetSubaccountNettingResponse.

tools.name: portfolio_get-subaccount-transfers
tools.description: Gets a paginated list of all transfers between subaccounts for the authenticated user. Optional: limit (default: 100), cursor. Returns the GetSubaccountTransfersResponse.

params.name: limit
params.description: Number of results per page. Defaults to 100.

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor. Use the cursor value returned from the previous response to get the next...

tools.name: portfolio_reset-order-group
tools.description: Resets the order group's matched contracts counter to zero, allowing new orders to be placed again after the limit was hit. Required: order_group_id. Optional: subaccount. Returns the updated EmptyResponse.

params.name: order_group_id
params.description: Order group ID

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: portfolio_trigger-order-group
tools.description: Triggers the order group, canceling all orders in the group and preventing new orders until the group is reset. Required: order_group_id. Optional: subaccount. Returns the updated EmptyResponse.

params.name: order_group_id
params.description: Order group ID

params.name: subaccount
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts). Defaults to 0.

tools.name: portfolio_update-order-group-limit
tools.description: Updates the order group contracts limit (rolling 15-second window). If the updated limit would immediately trigger the group, all orders in the group are canceled and the group is triggered. Required: order_group_id. Optional: contracts_limit, contracts_limit_fp. Returns the updated EmptyResponse.

params.name: order_group_id
params.description: Order group ID

params.name: contracts_limit
params.description: New maximum number of contracts that can be matched within this group over a rolling 15-second window. Whole contracts only. Provide contracts_limit or contracts_limit_fp; if both provided they must match.

params.name: contracts_limit_fp
params.description: Fixed-point contract count string (2 decimals, e.g., '10.00'; referred to as 'fp' in field names). Requests accept 0–2 decimal places (e.g., '10', '10.0', '10.00'); responses always emit 2 decimals. Fractional contract values (e.g., '2.50') are supported on markets with fractional trading enabled; the minimum granularity is 0.01 contracts. Integer contract count fields are legacy and will be deprecated; when both integer and fp fields are provided, they must match.

tools.name: portfolio_update-subaccount-netting
tools.description: Updates the netting enabled setting for a specific subaccount. Use 0 for the primary account, or 1-32 for numbered subaccounts. Required: enabled, subaccount_number.

params.name: enabled
params.description: Whether netting is enabled for this subaccount.

params.name: subaccount_number
params.description: Subaccount number (0 for primary, 1-32 for subaccounts).

tools.name: series_get
tools.description: Endpoint for getting data about a specific series by its ticker. A series represents a template for recurring events that follow the same format and rules (e.g., 'Monthly Jobs Report', 'Weekly Initial Jobless Claims', 'Daily Weather in NYC'). Series define the structure, settlement sources, and metadata that will be applied to each recurring event instance within that series. Required: series_ticker. Optional: include_volume (default: false). Returns the GetSeriesResponse.

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: The ticker of the series to retrieve

params.name: include_volume
params.description: If true, includes the total volume traded across all events in this series.

tools.name: series_get-fee-changes
tools.description: Get Series Fee Changes. Optional: series_ticker, show_historical (default: false). Returns the GetSeriesFeeChangesResponse.

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: Series ticker

params.name: show_historical
params.description: Show historical

tools.name: series_get-list
tools.description: Endpoint for getting data about multiple series with specified filters. A series represents a template for recurring events that follow the same format and rules (e.g., 'Monthly Jobs Report', 'Weekly Initial Jobless Claims', 'Daily Weather in NYC'). This endpoint allows you to browse and discover available series templates by category. Optional: category, tags, include_product_metadata (default: false) (plus 2 more). Returns the GetSeriesListResponse.

params.name: category
params.description: Category

params.name: tags
params.description: Tags

params.name: include_product_metadata
params.description: Include product metadata

params.name: include_volume
params.description: If true, includes the total volume traded across all events in each series.

params.name: min_updated_ts
params.description: Filter series with metadata updated after this Unix timestamp (in seconds). Use this to efficiently poll for changes.

tools.name: series_events_get-forecast-percentiles-history
tools.description: Endpoint for getting the historical raw and formatted forecast numbers for an event at specific percentiles. Required: ticker, series_ticker, percentiles, start_ts, end_ts, period_interval. Returns the GetEventForecastPercentilesHistoryResponse.

params.name: ticker
params.description: The event ticker

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: The series ticker

params.name: percentiles
params.description: Array of percentile values to retrieve (0-10000, max 10 values)

params.name: start_ts
params.description: Start timestamp for the range

params.name: end_ts
params.description: End timestamp for the range

params.name: period_interval
params.description: Specifies the length of each forecast period, in minutes. 0 for 5-second intervals, or 1, 60, or 1440 for minute-based intervals.

tools.name: series_events_get-market-candlesticks-by
tools.description: End-point for returning aggregated data across all markets corresponding to an event. Required: ticker, series_ticker, start_ts, end_ts, period_interval. Returns the GetEventCandlesticksResponse.

params.name: ticker
params.description: The event ticker

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: The series ticker

params.name: start_ts
params.description: Start timestamp for the range

params.name: end_ts
params.description: End timestamp for the range

params.name: period_interval
params.description: Specifies the length of each candlestick period, in minutes. Must be one minute, one hour, or one day.

tools.name: series_markets_get-candlesticks
tools.description: Time period length of each candlestick in minutes. Valid values: 1 (1 minute), 60 (1 hour), 1440 (1 day). Candlesticks for markets that settled before the historical cutoff are only available via `GET /historical/markets/{ticker}/candlesticks`. See [Historical Data](https://kalshi.com/docs/getting_started/historical_data) for details. Required: series_ticker, ticker, start_ts, end_ts, period_interval. Optional: include_latest_before_start (default: false). Returns the GetMarketCandlesticksResponse.

params.name: series_ticker
params.description: Series ticker - the series that contains the target market

params.name: ticker
params.description: Market ticker - unique identifier for the specific market

params.name: start_ts
params.description: Start timestamp (Unix timestamp). Candlesticks will include those ending on or after this time.

params.name: end_ts
params.description: End timestamp (Unix timestamp). Candlesticks will include those ending on or before this time.

params.name: period_interval
params.description: Time period length of each candlestick in minutes. Valid values are 1 (1 minute), 60 (1 hour), or 1440 (1 day).

params.name: include_latest_before_start
params.description: If true, prepends the latest candlestick available before the start_ts. This synthetic candlestick is created by: 1. Finding the most recent real candlestick before start_ts 2. Projecting it forward to the first period boundary (calculated as the next period interval after start_ts) 3. Setting all OHLC prices to null, and `previous_price` to the close price from the real candlestick

tools.name: structured-targets_get
tools.description: Page size (min: 1, max: 2000). Optional: ids, type, competition (plus 2 more). Returns the GetStructuredTargetsResponse.

params.name: ids
params.description: Filter by specific structured target IDs. Pass multiple IDs by repeating the parameter (e.g. `?ids=uuid1&ids=uuid2`).

params.name: type
params.description: Filter by structured target type

params.name: competition
params.description: Filter by competition. Matches against the league, conference, division, or tour in the structured target details.

params.name: page_size
params.description: Number of items per page (min 1, max 2000, default 100)

params.name: cursor
params.description: Pagination cursor

tools.name: structured-targets_get-structuredtargets
tools.description: Endpoint for getting data about a specific structured target by its ID. Required: structured_target_id. Returns the GetStructuredTargetResponse.

params.name: structured_target_id
params.description: Structured target ID
